page: Amazon Web Services m6i.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance in single-core operations, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1575.0 and a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1885.19. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics trend lower, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 3341.0 and a large-block memory read bandwidth of 29.35 GB/sec. Cryptographic capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated instructions, yielding average performance in Passmark CPU encryption tests. Static web serving throughput reaches an average of 6.51 GB/sec, while LLM inference using a small 135M model achieves 99.41 tokens/sec for text generation.

The m6i.xlarge offers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads where extreme multi-core throughput is not the primary requirement. Given its dedicated CPU allocation and standard memory-to-core ratio, it provides a stable environment for web serving, text processing, and light database operations. The lack of bundled local storage means storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, which should be factored into the overall resource architecture.
description: Amazon Web Services m6i.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, paired with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1575.0, alongside average performance in static web serving and cryptographic tasks. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively weak. This instance is best suited for balanced workloads such as static web serving, text processing, and light application hosting that do not require high-density local storage or intensive multi-threaded compute.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, suitable for balanced web serving and text processing workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 2 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and balanced memory configuration for standard web serving and application workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps without local storage or GPU hardware
Demonstrates average single-core performance and static web serving throughput of 6.51 GB/sec
Features hardware-accelerated cryptographic instructions including AES and SHA extensions
Suited for general-purpose workloads, text processing, and light application hosting
